name: ExtensionMedicinalProductDefinition_Route
title: R5: The path by which the product is taken into or makes contact with the body (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MedicinalProductDefinition.route` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MedicinalProductDefinition.route` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MedicinalProductDefinition.route` 0..* `CodeableConcept`
*  R4B: `MedicinalProductDefinition.route` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MedicinalProductDefinition.route` has a context of MedicinalProduct based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MedicinalProduct`.
Element `MedicinalProductDefinition.route` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The path by which the product is taken into or makes contact with the body (new)
element.comment:
Element `MedicinalProductDefinition.route` has a context of MedicinalProduct based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MedicinalProduct`.
Element `MedicinalProductDefinition.route` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: The path by which the product is taken into or makes contact with the body
element.definition: The path by which the product is taken into or makes contact with the body. In some regions this is referred to as the licenced or approved route. See also AdministrableProductDefinition resource. MedicinalProductDefinition.route is the same concept as AdministrableProductDefinition.routeOfAdministration.code, and they cannot be used together.
